Q: How does the angle of the shots affect the perception of the building's height?
A: The angle of the shots in the video affects the perception of the building's height by creating an optical illusion. When the camera is positioned at a lower angle, looking upwards towards the building, it can make the building appear taller and more imposing. This is because the perspective exaggerates the vertical lines of the building, making it seem as if it stretches further into the sky. Conversely, if the camera is positioned at a higher angle, looking downwards, the building may appear shorter and less prominent. In the video, the shots are taken from a low angle, which enhances the perceived height of the building, making it seem more grand and majestic.
C: The angle of the shots, taken from a lower perspective, enhances the building's height, making it appear more towering and prominent.
